name: claude-code-templates
description: Boilerplate templates for Claude Code extensions. Triggers on: create agent, new skill, command template, hook script, extension scaffold.
capabilities: Provides agent template with focus areas, principles, and quality checklist structure
Includes skill template following AgentSkills.io specification with YAML frontmatter
Offers command template with execution flow diagrams and option documentation
Supplies secure hook script template with input validation and error handling
Documents standard file paths and naming conventions for all extension types
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation templates for building Claude Code extensions. All static findings are false positives - the flagged patterns are template placeholders (backticks in markdown code blocks), example code showing how to write secure hooks, and references to standard Claude Code configuration paths. No executable code runs when this skill is used.
valueStatement: Building Claude Code extensions requires understanding multiple file formats and configuration patterns. This skill provides tested templates for agents, skills, commands, and hooks that you can customize immediately.
userTitle: Create Claude Code Extensions with Ready-Made Templates
limitations: Templates require manual customization for specific use cases
Does not auto-generate extensions from descriptions
Hook script template requires bash and jq to be installed

useCases.title: Create a New Expert Agent
useCases.description: Use the agent template to build a specialized persona with defined focus areas, quality checklist, and reference documentation.

useCases.title: Build a Claude Code Skill
useCases.description: Start with the skill template that includes proper YAML frontmatter, trigger keywords, and follows the AgentSkills.io specification.

useCases.title: Implement Secure Hook Scripts
useCases.description: Copy the hook template with built-in input validation, JSON parsing, and security patterns for PreToolUse or PostToolUse events.

promptTemplates.title: Get Template Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: What Claude Code templates are available and when should I use each one?

promptTemplates.title: Create Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a Python expert agent using the agent template. I want to focus on FastAPI and async patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Create Skill
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a new skill with proper structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill for git workflow automation using the skill template. Include common patterns for branching and PR reviews.

promptTemplates.title: Create Secure Hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a PreToolUse hook that validates Write operations to prevent modifications to package.json files.

bestPractices: Match the name field in YAML frontmatter exactly to the filename for proper discovery
Include trigger keywords in skill descriptions so Claude can find and activate them
Use the references directory for detailed documentation to keep SKILL.md under 500 lines
antiPatterns: Do not include sensitive data or credentials in template files
Avoid creating hooks without input validation as shown in the template
Do not skip the YAML frontmatter as it is required for Claude Code to recognize extensions

faq.question: Where should I put agent files?
faq.answer: Place agent files in ~/.claude/agents/ with the .md extension. The filename should match the name field in the YAML frontmatter.

faq.question: How do I make a hook script work?
faq.answer: Copy the template to .claude/hooks/, make it executable with chmod +x, then add the hook configuration to your settings.json or settings.local.json file.

faq.question: What is the AgentSkills.io specification?
faq.answer: It is an open standard for agent skills that defines required fields like name and description. The skill template follows this specification for compatibility.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for project-specific extensions?
faq.answer: Yes. Place project-specific files in .claude/ subdirectories within your project instead of ~/.claude/ for global extensions.

faq.question: Why does the hook template require jq?
faq.answer: Claude Code passes JSON data to hooks via stdin. The jq tool safely parses this JSON to extract fields like tool_name and tool_input.

faq.question: How do I test if my extension is working?
faq.answer: For agents, ask Claude to use your agent by name. For skills, mention trigger keywords. For commands, type the slash command. For hooks, check the log file.
